plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate limits.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% for greater income and portfolio stability.
Reduce SMH and CRWD exposure to 10% each to lower sector and single-name concentration.
Initiate a small position in AAPL at 5% for quality diversification within the portfolio.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation by reducing leverage and high-beta exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets a modest ~1.5%/month, focusing on capital preservation. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns, but also limited upside potential. The primary risk is underperforming in strong bull markets.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits and free up capital.
Reduce SOXL to a small 2% position and sell SPAL to lower overall leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim QQQI to 15% to rebalance the portfolio more towards growth opportunities.
Reduce SMH to 10% to diversify away from heavy semiconductor concentration.
Buy AVGO at 8% and AAPL at 5% to add quality tech names from the scanner list.
This plan balances risk and growth, maintaining a small leveraged ETF presence.
balanced.read: This balanced strategy aims for ~2.5%/month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It seeks a blend of broad market exposure and quality tech, with reduced but some leveraged ETF presence. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns, with potential for solid returns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits while maintaining broad exposure.
Increase SOXL to 10% to lean into leveraged semiconductor exposure, accepting higher risk.
Sell SPAL to consolidate leveraged ETF exposure into higher conviction plays.
Reduce QQQI to 10% to shift capital from income towards aggressive growth opportunities.
Trim CRWD to 10% to manage single-name concentration.
Buy AVGO at 12% and GUSH at 8% to add high-scoring scanner setups with strong risk/reward.
aggressive.read: This aggressive growth strategy targets ~4%/month by leveraging high-conviction setups. Expect significantly higher volatility and potential for large drawdowns due to increased leverage and concentration. Leveraged ETFs like SOXL decay daily, amplifying both gains and losses. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and free capital for income-focused assets.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate high-beta leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 30% to maximize monthly distribution potential.
Sell SMH and CRWD to reduce exposure to high-beta sectors and single names.
Trim QQQ to 10% to further shift the portfolio towards stability and income.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown over growth.
income.read: This income strategy targets ~1%/month, focusing on reliable monthly distributions and low drawdown. It prioritizes stability over growth, with minimal exposure to high-beta assets. While aiming for steady income, distributions are variable and NAV can still decline in market downturns; this is not a savings account.
